title: edit.rs silently ignores replace_all argument (admitted gap, no error)
description:
## Severity
Major

## Dimension
Functional Correctness / KISS / SOLID-L (LSP — interface promises something it does not deliver)

## Location
src/tools/file/edit.rs:84-95 (and tests at lines 258-272 admit the behavior)

## Violation
`execute_edit_file` accepts a `replace_all` arg (per the schema seen in tests `args.insert("replace_all", ...)`), but the body silently ignores it and unconditionally calls `replacen(_, _, 1)`:

```rust
let count = content.matches(old_string).count();
if count > 1 {
    return (format!("Found {count} occurrences of the text. Please provide a more specific old_string..."), true);
}
...
let new_content = content.replacen(old_string, new_string, 1);
```

The test `edit_replace_all_true_with_multi_occurrence_currently_errors` (line 259) explicitly documents that the flag is "silently ignored for N>1." This is the LSP violation in §3.2 — the tool's advertised contract diverges from its delivered behavior. Worse, it returns an error to the model that says "provide a more specific old_string" even when the model has set the explicit override that should have allowed bulk replacement.

## Mandated refactor
Read the `replace_all` arg; when `true`, use `content.replace(old, new)` (replace all occurrences) and report the count replaced. When `false` (or absent), keep the current single-replace + multi-occurrence-error path. Update the error path to suggest "either narrow old_string OR set replace_all: true." No `#[allow]` — fix the logic. Cross-references the gap noted in pinned test at edit.rs:259-272 ("gap #569").

## Filed from QA review dispatch
